vocabulary.definition: A visual or mathematical representation used to solve a problem
vocabulary.visual: A bar model or equation that represents a word problem

vocabulary.definition: A math sentence that uses an equal sign to show two expressions have the same value
The answer to an equation or problem
Checking whether an answer makes sense in the context of the problem
vocabulary.visual: If you divide 3 by 1/4, an answer of 12 is reasonable because 12 > 3

launch.narrative: Agent Torres is closing the biggest case of the year. She has 2/3 of a gallon of fingerprint dust left and each test uses 1/6 of a gallon. Before the evidence expires, she needs to figure out exactly how many tests she can run. Can you write the equation and solve it?
launch.contextImage: Detective at a forensics station with vials of fingerprint dust and case files spread across the desk
noticePrompts: What is the total amount of fingerprint dust?
How much does each test use?
What operation will help solve this problem?
wonderPrompts: How do you decide which number is the dividend and which is the divisor?
How can you check if your answer is reasonable?

explore.instructions: Read each word problem. Set up the fraction division equation, solve it, and check for reasonableness.
explore.label: Set Up and Solve Fraction Division Equations

rows.problem: A detective has 3/4 pound of plaster. Each mold uses 1/8 pound. How many molds can she make?
rows.check: Yes — 6 > 3/4 because each piece is very small

rows.problem: A suspect traveled 5/6 of a mile. Each city block is 1/12 of a mile. How many blocks did the suspect travel?
rows.check: Yes — 10 > 5/6 because twelfths are smaller than sixths

rows.problem: An evidence bag holds 2/3 of a kilogram. Each sample weighs 1/9 of a kilogram. How many samples fit?
rows.check: Yes — 6 > 2/3 because ninths are much smaller than thirds

discourse.prompt: How do you decide which value is the dividend and which is the divisor in a word problem?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The total amount is the ___ because it is being split into groups. The size of each group is the ___ because we are dividing by that amount.

approaching.stem: A ribbon is 4/5 of a yard long. Each bow uses 1/10 of a yard. Which equation finds how many bows can be made?
approaching.explanation: The total (4/5 yard) is divided into groups of 1/10 yard each. The equation is 4/5 ÷ 1/10 = 4/5 × 10/1 = 40/5 = 8 bows.

approaching.stem: A detective has 1/2 gallon of solution. Each test uses 1/8 gallon. How many tests can be run?

onLevel.label: Solve each word problem by writing and solving an equation

rows.problem: A board is 7/8 foot long. Each shelf bracket needs 1/4 foot. How many brackets fit?
A jug holds 5/6 liter. Each cup holds 1/3 liter. How many cups can be filled?
A path is 3/4 mile long. A runner does laps of 3/8 mile. How many laps fit?

extending.title: Find the Problem-Solving Error

workedExample.label: Problem
workedExample.work: A tank has 3/4 gallon of water. Each bucket holds 1/6 gallon. How many buckets can be filled?

extending.correctWork: The equation should be 3/4 ÷ 1/6 (total ÷ group size). Then 3/4 × 6/1 = 18/4 = 4 1/2 buckets.
hints: Which value is the total being divided — 3/4 or 1/6?
The student wrote the dividend and divisor in the wrong order. The total (3/4) should come first.

connect.scenario: A forensic chemist has 5/6 of a liter of testing solution. Each DNA test requires 1/12 of a liter. She needs to figure out how many tests she can run before the solution runs out.
connect.promptQuestion: How does setting up a fraction division equation help solve this real-world problem?
connect.prompt: The chemist can run ___ tests because 5/6 ÷ 1/12 = ___.

exitTicket.stem: A pipe is 3/4 meter long. Each connector piece is 3/8 meter. How many connectors fit on the pipe?
exitTicket.explanation: 3/4 ÷ 3/8 = 3/4 × 8/3 = 24/12 = 2. Two connector pieces fit on the pipe.
